mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or regime-specific IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand that translates into immediate equity price appreciation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters with above-trend GDP growth, or rolling predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 3.0 percent indicate a restrictive monetary stance that caps speculative leverage and stabilizes valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are negative in >60% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales and housing starts during a post-recession recovery phase confirm demand normalization, triggering immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances within the specified regime, or regime-filtered IC drops below 0.01.
